filter_index.video_title: Why We Love Kuari Pass Trek | A Tribute by Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow present from base camp towards January; entire landscape and valley white from December to end of March; upper reaches of Gorson and Kuari still under snow in spring; Khullara and Chitrakantha meadows usually under snow until mid-April
July and August are peak rainy season in Uttarakhand and trek is not accessible; mild rains start by end of summer (afternoon to evening) marking the beginning of monsoon
temperature_notes: Winter: Day 8–14 °C, Night -8 to -14 °C; notoriously cold, colder than other winter treks; 5 warm layers required
Spring: Day 13–17 °C, Night drops to zero; 3–4 warm layers
Summer: Day 18–24 °C, Night drops 3–8 °C; 3 warm layers
Autumn: Day 10–15 °C, Night drops to zero; 3–4 warm layers; nights get much colder as winter approaches

terrain_challenges: Steep ascent on day 1 of about 1500 ft over 2.75 km
Overhanging narrow trail for about 1 km overlooking Dhak valley between Tali and Gorson Bugyal, tricky in winter or rainy days
Unmarked trail entry into pine forests of Auli from Gorson Bugyal, easy to go astray, tricky on misty/foggy days with low visibility
Day 1 steep ascent of ~1500 ft over 2.75 km
Daily ascents and descents across four trekking days covering ~22 km with cumulative gain of ~4,731 ft (7,785 ft to 12,516 ft)
altitude_or_weather_risks: Day 1 steep ascent of ~1500 ft over 2.75 km can increase susceptibility to Acute Mountain Sickness
Trek reaches 12,500 ft; above 10,000 ft AMS risk is real for both first-time and experienced trekkers
Even fit persons climbing too high too soon are at equal risk of AMS
Prolonged rainfall or snowfall can change trek dynamics; Tali campsite is extremely prone to flooding
Rain can make forest trails slushy and unsafe to walk on

fitness_benchmark: Must be able to walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level).
Runs must meet the trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month.
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria.
prep_window: Screenshots must be uploaded 15 to 20 days before the trek date.
Runs must meet the fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month.

experience_themes: Winter: snow experience, mighty mountain views, dreamy wonderland with pines/oak/dwarf rhododendrons, soft powdery snowflakes on pine trees, possible snowfall and snowball fights
Spring: last of winter snow, receding snow at lower campsites, green meadows, blooming flowers and rhododendrons, colour contrasts of snow/greenery/flowers
Summer: bright sunny days, mildly colder nights, comfortable trekking temperatures
Autumn: crisp mountain views, golden brown meadows, colour of the season
Winter: unending vista of the biggest mountains in India opening up from day one; white snow-draped landscape
Spring: colour contrasts of snow, greenery and flowers on meadows
primary_watchouts: Winter: snow raises trek difficulty by a notch; prepare well; notoriously cold
End of summer: mild rains in afternoons/evenings marking monsoon onset
Autumn: ending trek towards Auli and crossing towards Gorson Bugyal depends on snow and weather forecast; in bad weather or unsafe ridge, trek leader may return to Khullara campsite
July and August due to peak rainy/monsoon season in Uttarakhand
Steep ascent on day 1 of about 1500 ft over 2.75 km
Overhanging narrow trail for about 1 km overlooking Dhak valley between Tali and Gorson Bugyal, tricky in winter or rainy days
best_for: Winter: snow lovers, mountain lovers seeking snow experience and mighty mountain views
Spring: those wanting last of winter snow with colour contrasts of meadows
Summer: trekkers seeking comfortable temperatures and bright sunny days
Autumn: trekkers seeking crisp mountain views and golden meadows
not_ideal_for: July and August due to peak rainy/monsoon season in Uttarakhand
